name: loop_slice_split
description: Loop Slice 'Split' option (task 0251). A unit cube (8 verts / 12 edges / 6 quad faces); the seed edge is the vertical edge (1,-1,-1)-(1,1,-1) shared by the front and right faces, so the ring cuts a horizontal loop around the 4 side faces at y=0 (4 vertical rails → 4 midpoints). With Split OFF (default, one connected loop, byte-for-byte) the cut stays watertight: the 4 rail midpoints are SHARED between the upper and lower sub-face of each side, so 12 verts / 20 edges / 10 faces, still one closed manifold shell. With Split ON each rail midpoint is DUPLICATED into two coincident verts (a lo copy toward the bottom, a hi copy toward the top), so the single connected loop becomes TWO distinct boundary edge-loops and the cube splits into two disconnected shells (top cap + bottom cap): 16 verts / 24 edges / 10 faces. The 4 extra verts are the 4 duplicated midpoints (verts 12-15 sit exactly on top of verts 8-11), and the 4 interior loop edges (shared, 2 faces each) become 8 boundary edges (1 face each) — proof the two sides are disconnected along the cut. Faces are unchanged (10) because splitting duplicates verts, it does not add faces. Reference-independent geometry law: every new vertex sits at the 0.5 midpoint of a pre-op vertical edge; the split duplicates coincide exactly with their connected originals (zero gap — Gap/task 0253 later moves them apart).
source: LIVE-captured from the reference app (task 0261) — driven headlessly through the reference app's command port (no mouse): build cube → select the vertical seed edge → activate Loop Slice → set count/position/split/caps via tool attributes → apply → drop → dump verts+faces. Full drive transcript + raw dumps in the private toolcard (toolcards/mesh.loopSlice/findings.md + capture/). The real geometry: Split OFF ⇒ one connected loop (12v/20e/10f, one closed shell); Split ON ⇒ each of the 4 rail midpoints duplicated into a coincident lo/hi pair, opening the surface into TWO disconnected boundary edge-loops (16v/24e/10f, two open shells), verts 12-15 coincident with 8-11. This matches vibe3d's prior implementation exactly — Split was already correct (the 0261 fix was the Cap topology, see loop_slice_caps.json). Coordinates scaled from the reference's ±0.5 cube to this ±1 fixture cube; topology captured verbatim.

provenance.notes: Driven headlessly via command port; matches vibe3d's prior Split implementation (the 0261 fix was Cap topology, not Split).
